steps.title: Find the radius
steps.content:
The radius is the distance from $C(h,k)$ to $A(h+2,k+\sqrt{237})$:
$$
r=\sqrt{(h+2-h)^2+(k+\sqrt{237}-k)^2}
$$
$$
r=\sqrt{2^2+(\sqrt{237})^2}=\sqrt{241}
$$

steps.title: Use the right angle
steps.content:
Both $CA$ and $CB$ are radii, so each has length $\sqrt{241}$. Since the angle at $C$ is a right angle, triangle $ACB$ is a right triangle with legs $CA$ and $CB$:
$$
AB^2=(\sqrt{241})^2+(\sqrt{241})^2=482
$$

steps.title: Find the length
steps.content: Take the positive square root because a length must be positive: $$ AB=\sqrt{482} $$ The answer is D.
